newsSince: Mitsubishi Electric shipped 5th-gen SiC-MOSFET bare-die samples, co-developed a new standard power-module package (3-level circuits) with Semikron Danfoss (Jun 8), will launch a free PCS design-data service on Jun 28, opened a Boston DX hub, and added an AI-robotics collaboration; FY27 guidance targets ¥6,200B revenue / ¥475B net profit. Incrementally constructive on power-electronics/automation, but mostly pre-June-9 or modest.

sources.label: Mitsubishi Electric power-semiconductor data-provision service (Business Wire)
Mitsubishi Electric 2027 guidance and power-semi push (Simply Wall St)
Mitsubishi Electric & Semikron Danfoss new power-module package (BusinessWire, Jun 8)
Mitsubishi Electric 2027 guidance & power-semiconductor push (Simply Wall St)

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha-vs-passives lens: early-June FY27 guidance, Semikron Danfoss power-module standard and June 28 power-semi data service are real power-electronics/automation positives, but Mitsubishi is bucketed 'secondary' - farther from the passive/power-supply bottleneck stack. $78B mega-cap; incremental, not thesis-changing. Hold rank 38.
verdicts.bull: Power-module standard with Semikron Danfoss, 8th-gen IGBT/5th-gen SiC, FY27 guidance and DX pivot broaden the power-electronics franchise.
verdicts.neutral: Useful AI-adjacent exposure but diversified mega-cap sits far from the passives choke point this page rewards.
verdicts.bear: $78B conglomerate dilutes any bottleneck alpha; news is incremental; residual alpha thin on this lens.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Power-semiconductor/SiC design adoption; FY27 guidance delivery
